en.extract:
The white-rumped spinetail or white-rumped needletail (Zoonavena sylvatica) is a species of swift found in the forests of Bangladesh, India (the Western Ghats) and Nepal. It is often seen over waterbodies in the middle of forest. It can resemble a house swift but has a white vent.


Diet
The white-rumped spinetail is insectivorous.

The '''white-rumped spinetail''' or '''white-rumped needletail''' ('''''Zoonavena sylvatica''''') is a species of swift found in the forests of [[Bangladesh]], [[India]] (the [[Western Ghats]]) and [[Nepal]]. It is often seen over waterbodies in the middle of forest. It can resemble a house swift but has a white vent.

== Diet ==
The white-rumped spinetail is insectivorous.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Upadhyaya |first=Laxmi Prasad |last2=Pandey |first2=Naresh |last3=Khanal |first3=Laxman |date=2022-03-01 |title=Tribhuvan University area serves as a greenspace for birds in the Kathmandu Valley, Central Nepal |journal=Journal of Animal Diversity |volume=4 |issue=1 |pages=27–40 |doi=10.52547/jad.2022.4.1.4 |issn=2676-685X|doi-access=free }}</ref>
